palette_hint: warm off-white background (#fdf6ec), dark charcoal text (#2b2b2b), terracotta accent (#c0522a) and muted sage green accent (#7a9e7e)
type_style: system serif (Georgia) for headings and recipe titles, system sans-serif for body text and measurements, medium weight, warm and unpretentious feel
description: A single hand-written-style recipe card site for a home cook sharing her definitive slow-braised lamb shoulder with preserved lemon — one dish presented across five focused pages.
constraints: single-column layout, all content centered with max-width of 620px
no images, no icons, no decorative elements — plain text and thin horizontal rules only
section headings in terracotta (#c0522a), all other text in charcoal (#2b2b2b)
thin 1px solid sage green (#7a9e7e) horizontal rules between every major section
top navigation is a single centered row of plain text links, active page underlined, no borders or backgrounds

page_specs.recipe: Displays the recipe title 'Slow-Braised Lamb Shoulder with Preserved Lemon & Olives' as a large serif headline, a one-line yield note ('Serves 4–6, prep 20 min, cook 4 hr'), and a two-sentence description of the dish's character and origin. A horizontal rule separates the header from a short paragraph on why this recipe matters to the cook.
page_specs.ingredients: A structured list of ingredients divided into two labeled groups: 'For the Braise' (9 items with precise quantities, e.g. '1.8 kg bone-in lamb shoulder') and 'For the Gremolata' (4 items). Each item is a plain text line; quantities are left-aligned and ingredient names follow. A note at the bottom recommends sourcing preserved lemons from a specific style of Moroccan brine.
page_specs.method: Numbered step-by-step instructions across 8 clearly written steps, each a short paragraph of 2–3 sentences. Steps cover searing, building the braise, oven temperature (160°C / 325°F), timing cues, and resting. A horizontal rule separates each step from the next for easy reading while cooking.
page_specs.notes: Three subsections — 'Substitutions', 'Make Ahead', and 'Serving Suggestions' — each with a short heading in terracotta and two to three sentences of practical advice. Includes a specific wine pairing recommendation (Grenache-based southern Rhône) and a note on reheating leftovers without drying the meat.
page_specs.about-cook: Two paragraphs introducing Miriam Osei-Bonsu, a home cook in Bristol who learned braising from her Ghanaian grandmother and later lived in Marrakech. Ends with her philosophy on slow cooking in one italicized sentence and a plain-text email address for recipe questions.
